1: 💎🔗 Langchain.rb
2: ---
3: ⚡ Building LLM-powered applications in Ruby ⚡
4: 
5: For deep Rails integration see: [langchainrb_rails](https://github.com/patterns-ai-core/langchainrb_rails) gem.
6: 
7: Available for paid consulting engagements! [Email me](mailto:andrei@sourcelabs.io).
8: 
9: ![Tests status](https://github.com/patterns-ai-core/langchainrb/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg?branch=main)
10: [![Gem Version](https://badge.fury.io/rb/langchainrb.svg)](https://badge.fury.io/rb/langchainrb)
11: [![Docs](http://img.shields.io/badge/yard-docs-blue.svg)](http://rubydoc.info/gems/langchainrb)
12: [![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT-green.svg)](https://github.com/patterns-ai-core/langchainrb/blob/main/LICENSE.txt)
13: [![](https://dcbadge.vercel.app/api/server/WDARp7J2n8?compact=true&style=flat)](https://discord.gg/WDARp7J2n8)
14: [![X](https://img.shields.io/twitter/url/https/twitter.com/cloudposse.svg?style=social&label=Follow%20%40rushing_andrei)](https://twitter.com/rushing_andrei)
15: 
16: ## Use Cases
17: * Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) and vector search
18: * [Assistants](#assistants) (chat bots)
19: 
20: ## Table of Contents
21: 
22: - [Installation](#installation)
23: - [Usage](#usage)
24: - [Unified Interface for LLMs](#unified-interface-for-llms)
25: - [Prompt Management](#prompt-management)
26: - [Output Parsers](#output-parsers)
27: - [Building RAG](#building-retrieval-augment-generation-rag-system)
28: - [Assistants](#assistants)
29: - [Evaluations](#evaluations-evals)
30: - [Examples](#examples)
31: - [Logging](#logging)
32: - [Problems](#problems)
33: - [Development](#development)
34: - [Discord](#discord)
35: 
36: ## Installation
37: 
38: Install the gem and add to the application's Gemfile by executing:
39: 
40:     bundle add langchainrb
41: 
42: If bundler is not being used to manage dependencies, install the gem by executing:
43: 
44:     gem install langchainrb
45: 
46: Additional gems may be required. They're not included by default so you can include only what you need.
47: 
48: ## Usage
49: 
50: ```ruby
51: require "langchain"
52: ```
53: 
54: # Unified Interface for LLMs
55: 
56: The `Langchain::LLM` module provides a unified interface for interacting with various Large Language Model (LLM) providers. This abstraction allows you to easily switch between different LLM backends without changing your application code.
57: 
58: ## Supported LLM Providers
59: 
60: - Anthropic
61: - AWS Bedrock
62: - Azure OpenAI
63: - Cohere
64: - Google Gemini
65: - Google Vertex AI
66: - HuggingFace
67: - Mistral AI
68: - Ollama
69: - OpenAI
70: - Replicate
71: 
72: ## Usage
73: 
74: All LLM classes inherit from `Langchain::LLM::Base` and provide a consistent interface for common operations:
75: 
76: 1. Generating embeddings
77: 2. Generating prompt completions
78: 3. Generating chat completions
79: 
80: ### Initialization
81: 
82: Most LLM classes can be initialized with an API key and optional default options:
83: 
84: ```ruby
85: llm = Langchain::LLM::OpenAI.new(
86:   api_key: ENV["OPENAI_API_KEY"],
87:   default_options: { temperature: 0.7, chat_model: "gpt-4o" }
88: )
89: ```
90: 
91: ### Generating Embeddings
92: 
93: Use the `embed` method to generate embeddings for given text:
94: 
95: ```ruby
96: response = llm.embed(text: "Hello, world!")
97: embedding = response.embedding
98: ```
99:

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Langchain
4:   # Assistants are Agent-like objects that leverage helpful instructions, LLMs, tools and knowledge to respond to user queries.
5:   # Assistants can be configured with an LLM of your choice, any vector search database and easily extended with additional tools.
6:   #
7:   # Usage:
8:   #     llm = Langchain::LLM::GoogleGemini.new(api_key: ENV["GOOGLE_GEMINI_API_KEY"])
9:   #     assistant = Langchain::Assistant.new(
10:   #       llm: llm,
11:   #       instructions: "You're a News Reporter AI",
12:   #       tools: [Langchain::Tool::NewsRetriever.new(api_key: ENV["NEWS_API_KEY"])]
13:   #     )
14:   class Assistant
15:     attr_reader :llm,
16:       :instructions,
17:       :state,
18:       :llm_adapter,
19:       :messages,
20:       :tool_choice,
21:       :total_prompt_tokens,
22:       :total_completion_tokens,
23:       :total_tokens
24: 
25:     attr_accessor :tools,
26:       :add_message_callback,
27:       :tool_execution_callback,
28:       :parallel_tool_calls
29: 
30:     # Create a new assistant
31:     #
32:     # @param llm [Langchain::LLM::Base] LLM instance that the assistant will use
33:     # @param tools [Array<Langchain::Tool::Base>] Tools that the assistant has access to
34:     # @param instructions [String] The system instructions
35:     # @param tool_choice [String] Specify how tools should be selected. Options: "auto", "any", "none", or <specific function name>
36:     # @param parallel_tool_calls [Boolean] Whether or not to run tools in parallel
37:     # @param messages [Array<Langchain::Assistant::Messages::Base>] The messages
38:     # @param add_message_callback [Proc] A callback function (Proc or lambda) that is called when any message is added to the conversation
39:     # @param tool_execution_callback [Proc] A callback function (Proc or lambda) that is called right before a tool function is executed
40:     def initialize(
41:       llm:,
42:       tools: [],
43:       instructions: nil,
44:       tool_choice: "auto",
45:       parallel_tool_calls: true,
46:       messages: [],
47:       # Callbacks
48:       add_message_callback: nil,
49:       tool_execution_callback: nil,
50:       &block
51:     )
52:       unless tools.is_a?(Array) && tools.all? { |tool| tool.class.singleton_class.include?(Langchain::ToolDefinition) }
53:         raise ArgumentError, "Tools must be an array of objects extending Langchain::ToolDefinition"
54:       end
55: 
56:       @llm = llm
57:       @llm_adapter = LLM::Adapter.build(llm)
58: 
59:       @add_message_callback = add_message_callback if validate_callback!("add_message_callback", add_message_callback)
60:       @tool_execution_callback = tool_execution_callback if validate_callback!("tool_execution_callback", tool_execution_callback)
61: 
62:       self.messages = messages
63:       @tools = tools
64:       @parallel_tool_calls = parallel_tool_calls
65:       self.tool_choice = tool_choice
66:       self.instructions = instructions
67:       @block = block
68:       @state = :ready
69: 
70:       @total_prompt_tokens = 0
71:       @total_completion_tokens = 0
72:       @total_tokens = 0
73:     end
74: 
75:     # Add a user message to the messages array
76:     #
77:     # @param role [String] The role attribute of the message. Default: "user"
78:     # @param content [String] The content of the message
79:     # @param image_url [String] The URL of the image to include in the message
80:     # @param tool_calls [Array<Hash>] The tool calls to include in the message
81:     # @param tool_call_id [String] The ID of the tool call to include in the message
82:     # @return [Array<Langchain::Message>] The messages
83:     def add_message(role: "user", content: nil, image_url: nil, tool_calls: [], tool_call_id: nil)
84:       message = build_message(role: role, content: content, image_url: image_url, tool_calls: tool_calls, tool_call_id: tool_call_id)
85: 
86:       # Call the callback with the message
87:       add_message_callback.call(message) if add_message_callback # rubocop:disable Style/SafeNavigation
88: 
89:       # Prepend the message to the messages array
90:       messages << message
91: 
92:       @state = :ready
93: 
94:       messages
95:     end
96: 
97:     # Convert messages to an LLM APIs-compatible array of hashes
98:     #
99:     # @return [Array<Hash>] Messages as an OpenAI API-compatible array of hashes
100:     def array_of_message_hashes
101:       messages
102:         .map(&:to_hash)
103:         .compact
104:     end
105: 
106:     # Only used by the Assistant when it calls the LLM#complete() method
107:     def prompt_of_concatenated_messages
108:       messages.map(&:to_s).join
109:     end
110: 
111:     # Set multiple messages
112:     #
113:     # @param messages [Array<Langchain::Message>] The messages to set
114:     # @return [Array<Langchain::Message>] The messages
115:     def messages=(messages)
116:       raise ArgumentError, "messages array must only contain Langchain::Message instance(s)" unless messages.is_a?(Array) && messages.all? { |m| m.is_a?(Messages::Base) }
117: 
118:       @messages = messages
119:     end
120: 
121:     # Add multiple messages
122:     #
123:     # @param messages [Array<Hash>] The messages to add
124:     # @return [Array<Langchain::Message>] The messages
125:     def add_messages(messages:)
126:       messages.each do |message_hash|
127:         add_message(**message_hash.slice(:content, :role, :tool_calls, :tool_call_id))
128:       end
129:     end
130: 
131:     # Run the assistant
132:     #
133:     # @param auto_tool_execution [Boolean] Whether or not to automatically run tools
134:     # @return [Array<Langchain::Message>] The messages
135:     def run(auto_tool_execution: false)
136:       if messages.empty?
137:         Langchain.logger.warn("#{self.class} - No messages to process")
138:         @state = :completed
139:         return
140:       end
141: 
142:       @state = :in_progress
143:       @state = handle_state until run_finished?(auto_tool_execution)
144: 
145:       messages
146:     end
147: 
148:     # Run the assistant with automatic tool execution
149:     #
150:     # @return [Array<Langchain::Message>] The messages
151:     def run!
152:       run(auto_tool_execution: true)
153:     end
154: 
155:     # Add a user message and run the assistant
156:     #
157:     # @param content [String] The content of the message
158:     # @param auto_tool_execution [Boolean] Whether or not to automatically run tools
159:     # @return [Array<Langchain::Message>] The messages
160:     def add_message_and_run(content: nil, image_url: nil, auto_tool_execution: false)
161:       add_message(content: content, image_url: image_url, role: "user")
162:       run(auto_tool_execution: auto_tool_execution)
163:     end
164: 
165:     # Add a user message and run the assistant with automatic tool execution
166:     #
167:     # @param content [String] The content of the message
168:     # @return [Array<Langchain::Message>] The messages
169:     def add_message_and_run!(content: nil, image_url: nil)
170:       add_message_and_run(content: content, image_url: image_url, auto_tool_execution: true)
171:     end
172: 
173:     # Submit tool output
174:     #
175:     # @param tool_call_id [String] The ID of the tool call to submit output for
176:     # @param output [String] The output of the tool
177:     # @return [Array<Langchain::Message>] The messages
178:     def submit_tool_output(tool_call_id:, output:)
179:       # TODO: Validate that `tool_call_id` is valid by scanning messages and checking if this tool call ID was invoked
180:       add_message(role: @llm_adapter.tool_role, content: output, tool_call_id: tool_call_id)
181:     end
182: 
183:     # Delete all messages
184:     #
185:     # @return [Array] Empty messages array
186:     def clear_messages!
187:       # TODO: If this a bug? Should we keep the "system" message?
188:       @messages = []
189:     end
190: 
191:     # Set new instructions
192:     #
193:     # @param new_instructions [String] New instructions that will be set as a system message
194:     # @return [Array<Langchain::Message>] The messages
195:     def instructions=(new_instructions)
196:       @instructions = new_instructions
197: 
198:       if @llm_adapter.support_system_message?
199:         # TODO: Should we still set a system message even if @instructions is "" or nil?
200:         replace_system_message!(content: new_instructions)
201:       end
202:     end
203: 
204:     # Set tool_choice, how tools should be selected
205:     #
206:     # @param new_tool_choice [String] Tool choice
207:     # @return [String] Selected tool choice
208:     def tool_choice=(new_tool_choice)
209:       validate_tool_choice!(new_tool_choice)
210:       @tool_choice = new_tool_choice
211:     end
212: 
213:     private
214: 
215:     # Replace old system message with new one
216:     #
217:     # @param content [String] New system message content
218:     # @return [Array<Langchain::Message>] The messages
219:     def replace_system_message!(content:)
220:       messages.delete_if(&:system?)
221:       return if content.nil?
222: 
223:       message = build_message(role: "system", content: content)
224:       messages.unshift(message)
225:     end
226: 
227:     # TODO: If tool_choice = "tool_function_name" and then tool is removed from the assistant, should we set tool_choice back to "auto"?
228:     def validate_tool_choice!(tool_choice)
229:       allowed_tool_choices = llm_adapter.allowed_tool_choices.concat(available_tool_names)
230:       unless allowed_tool_choices.include?(tool_choice)
231:         raise ArgumentError, "Tool choice must be one of: #{allowed_tool_choices.join(", ")}"
232:       end
233:     end
234: 
235:     # Check if the run is finished
236:     #
237:     # @param auto_tool_execution [Boolean] Whether or not to automatically run tools
238:     # @return [Boolean] Whether the run is finished
239:     def run_finished?(auto_tool_execution)
240:       finished_states = [:completed, :failed]
241: 
242:       requires_manual_action = (@state == :requires_action) && !auto_tool_execution
243:       finished_states.include?(@state) || requires_manual_action
244:     end
245: 
246:     # Handle the current state and transition to the next state
247:     #
248:     # @return [Symbol] The next state
249:     def handle_state
250:       case @state
251:       when :in_progress
252:         process_latest_message
253:       when :requires_action
254:         execute_tools
255:       end
256:     end
257: 
258:     # Process the latest message
259:     #
260:     # @return [Symbol] The next state
261:     def process_latest_message
262:       last_message = messages.last
263: 
264:       case last_message.standard_role
265:       when :system
266:         handle_system_message
267:       when :llm
268:         handle_llm_message
269:       when :user, :tool
270:         handle_user_or_tool_message
271:       else
272:         handle_unexpected_message
273:       end
274:     end
275: 
276:     # Handle system message scenario
277:     #
278:     # @return [Symbol] The completed state
279:     def handle_system_message
280:       Langchain.logger.warn("#{self.class} - At least one user message is required after a system message")
281:       :completed
282:     end
283: 
284:     # Handle LLM message scenario
285:     #
286:     # @return [Symbol] The next state
287:     def handle_llm_message
288:       messages.last.tool_calls.any? ? :requires_action : :completed
289:     end
290: 
291:     # Handle unexpected message scenario
292:     #
293:     # @return [Symbol] The failed state
294:     def handle_unexpected_message
295:       Langchain.logger.error("#{self.class} - Unexpected message role encountered: #{messages.last.standard_role}")
296:       :failed
297:     end
298: 
299:     # Handle user or tool message scenario by processing the LLM response
300:     #
301:     # @return [Symbol] The next state
302:     def handle_user_or_tool_message
303:       response = chat_with_llm
304: 
305:       add_message(role: response.role, content: response.chat_completion, tool_calls: response.tool_calls)
306:       record_used_tokens(response.prompt_tokens, response.completion_tokens, response.total_tokens)
307: 
308:       set_state_for(response: response)
309:     end
310: 
311:     def set_state_for(response:)
312:       if response.tool_calls.any?
313:         :in_progress
314:       elsif response.chat_completion
315:         :completed
316:       elsif response.completion # Currently only used by Ollama
317:         :completed
318:       else
319:         Langchain.logger.error("#{self.class} - LLM response does not contain tool calls, chat or completion response")
320:         :failed
321:       end
322:     end
323: 
324:     # Execute the tools based on the tool calls in the last message
325:     #
326:     # @return [Symbol] The next state
327:     def execute_tools
328:       run_tools(messages.last.tool_calls)
329:       :in_progress
330:     rescue => e
331:       Langchain.logger.error("#{self.class} - Error running tools: #{e.message}; #{e.backtrace.join('\n')}")
332:       :failed
333:     end
334: 
335:     # Call to the LLM#chat() method
336:     #
337:     # @return [Langchain::LLM::BaseResponse] The LLM response object
338:     def chat_with_llm
339:       Langchain.logger.debug("#{self.class} - Sending a call to #{llm.class}")
340: 
341:       params = @llm_adapter.build_chat_params(
342:         instructions: @instructions,
343:         messages: array_of_message_hashes,
344:         tools: @tools,

346:         parallel_tool_calls: parallel_tool_calls
347:       )

352:     #

354:     def run_tools(tool_calls)

356:       tool_calls.each do |tool_call|

361:     # Run the tool call
362:     #
363:     # @param tool_call [Hash] The tool call to run

365:     def run_tool(tool_call)
366:       tool_call_id, tool_name, method_name, tool_arguments = @llm_adapter.extract_tool_call_args(tool_call: tool_call)
367: 
368:       tool_instance = tools.find do |t|

370:       end or raise ArgumentError, "Tool: #{tool_name} not found in assistant.tools"
371: 
372:       # Call the callback if set
373:       tool_execution_callback.call(tool_call_id, tool_name, method_name, tool_arguments) if tool_execution_callback # rubocop:disable Style/SafeNavigation
374: 
375:       output = tool_instance.public_send(method_name, **tool_arguments)
376: 
377:       # Handle both ToolResponse and legacy return values
378:       if output.is_a?(ToolResponse)
379:         add_message(role: @llm_adapter.tool_role, content: output.content, image_url: output.image_url, tool_call_id: tool_call_id)
380:       else
381:         submit_tool_output(tool_call_id: tool_call_id, output: output)
382:       end

3: module Langchain
4:   class Assistant

7:         # OpenAI uses the following roles:
8:         ROLES = [
9:           "system",
10:           "assistant",
11:           "user",
12:           "tool"
13:         ].freeze
14: 
15:         TOOL_ROLE = "tool"
16: 
17:         # Initialize a new OpenAI message
18:         #
19:         # @param role [String] The role of the message
20:         # @param content [String] The content of the message
21:         # @param image_url [String] The URL of the image
22:         # @param tool_calls [Array<Hash>] The tool calls made in the message
23:         # @param tool_call_id [String] The ID of the tool call
24:         def initialize(
25:           role:,
26:           content: nil,
27:           image_url: nil,
28:           tool_calls: [],
29:           tool_call_id: nil
30:         )
31:           raise ArgumentError, "Role must be one of #{ROLES.join(", ")}" unless ROLES.include?(role)
32:           raise ArgumentError, "Tool calls must be an array of hashes" unless tool_calls.is_a?(Array) && tool_calls.all? { |tool_call| tool_call.is_a?(Hash) }
33: 
34:           @role = role
35:           # Some Tools return content as a JSON hence `.to_s`
36:           @content = content.to_s
37:           @image_url = image_url
38:           @tool_calls = tool_calls
39:           @tool_call_id = tool_call_id
40:         end
41: 
42:         # Check if the message came from an LLM
43:         #
44:         # @return [Boolean] true/false whether this message was produced by an LLM
45:         def llm?
46:           assistant?
47:         end
48: 
49:         # Convert the message to an OpenAI API-compatible hash
50:         #
51:         # @return [Hash] The message as an OpenAI API-compatible hash
52:         def to_hash
53:           if assistant?
54:             assistant_hash
55:           elsif system?
56:             system_hash
57:           elsif tool?
58:             tool_hash
59:           elsif user?
60:             user_hash
61:           end
62:         end
63: 
64:         # Check if the message came from an LLM
65:         #
66:         # @return [Boolean] true/false whether this message was produced by an LLM
67:         def assistant?
68:           role == "assistant"
69:         end
70: 
71:         # Check if the message are system instructions
72:         #
73:         # @return [Boolean] true/false whether this message are system instructions
74:         def system?
75:           role == "system"
76:         end
77: 
78:         # Check if the message is a tool call
79:         #
80:         # @return [Boolean] true/false whether this message is a tool call
81:         def tool?
82:           role == "tool"
83:         end
84: 
85:         def user?
86:           role == "user"
87:         end
88: 
89:         # Convert the message to an OpenAI API-compatible hash
90:         # @return [Hash] The message as an OpenAI API-compatible hash, with the role as "assistant"
91:         def assistant_hash
92:           if tool_calls.any?
93:             {
94:               role: "assistant",
95:               tool_calls: tool_calls
96:             }
97:           else
98:             {
99:               role: "assistant",
100:               content: build_content_array
101:             }
102:           end
103:         end
104: 
105:         # Convert the message to an OpenAI API-compatible hash
106:         # @return [Hash] The message as an OpenAI API-compatible hash, with the role as "system"
107:         def system_hash
108:           {
109:             role: "system",
110:             content: build_content_array
111:           }
112:         end
113: 
114:         # Convert the message to an OpenAI API-compatible hash
115:         # @return [Hash] The message as an OpenAI API-compatible hash, with the role as "tool"
116:         def tool_hash
117:           {
118:             role: "tool",
119:             tool_call_id: tool_call_id,
120:             content: build_content_array
121:           }
122:         end
123: 
124:         # Convert the message to an OpenAI API-compatible hash
125:         # @return [Hash] The message as an OpenAI API-compatible hash, with the role as "user"
126:         def user_hash
127:           {
128:             role: "user",
129:             content: build_content_array
130:           }
131:         end
132: 
133:         # Builds the content value for the message hash
134:         # @return [Array<Hash>] An array of content hashes, with keys :type and :text or :image_url.
135:         def build_content_array
136:           content_details = []
137:           if content && !content.empty?
138:             content_details << {
139:               type: "text",
140:               text: content
141:             }
142:           end
143: 
144:           if image_url
145:             content_details << {
146:               type: "image_url",
147:               image_url: {
148:                 url: image_url
149:               }
150:             }
151:           end
152:           content_details
153:         end
154:       end
155:     end
156:   end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require "openai"
4: 
5: module Langchain::LLM
6:   # LLM interface for OpenAI APIs: https://platform.openai.com/overview
7:   #
8:   # Gem requirements:
9:   #    gem "ruby-openai", "~> 6.3.0"
10:   #
11:   # Usage:
12:   #    llm = Langchain::LLM::OpenAI.new(
13:   #      api_key: ENV["OPENAI_API_KEY"],
14:   #      llm_options: {}, # Available options: https://github.com/alexrudall/ruby-openai/blob/main/lib/openai/client.rb#L5-L13
15:   #      default_options: {}
16:   #    )
17:   class OpenAI < Base
18:     DEFAULTS = {
19:       n: 1,
20:       chat_model: "gpt-4o-mini",
21:       embedding_model: "text-embedding-3-small"
22:     }.freeze
23: 
24:     EMBEDDING_SIZES = {
25:       "text-embedding-ada-002" => 1536,
26:       "text-embedding-3-large" => 3072,
27:       "text-embedding-3-small" => 1536
28:     }.freeze
29: 
30:     # Initialize an OpenAI LLM instance
31:     #
32:     # @param api_key [String] The API key to use
33:     # @param client_options [Hash] Options to pass to the OpenAI::Client constructor
34:     def initialize(api_key:, llm_options: {}, default_options: {})
35:       depends_on "ruby-openai", req: "openai"
36: 
37:       llm_options[:log_errors] = Langchain.logger.debug? unless llm_options.key?(:log_errors)
38: 
39:       @client = ::OpenAI::Client.new(access_token: api_key, **llm_options) do |f|
40:         f.response :logger, Langchain.logger, {headers: true, bodies: true, errors: true}
41:       end
42: 
43:       @defaults = DEFAULTS.merge(default_options)
44:       chat_parameters.update(
45:         model: {default: @defaults[:chat_model]},
46:         logprobs: {},
47:         top_logprobs: {},
48:         n: {default: @defaults[:n]},
49:         temperature: {default: @defaults[:temperature]},
50:         user: {},
51:         response_format: {default: @defaults[:response_format]}
52:       )
53:       chat_parameters.ignore(:top_k)
54:     end
55: 
56:     # Generate an embedding for a given text
57:     #
58:     # @param text [String] The text to generate an embedding for
59:     # @param model [String] ID of the model to use
60:     # @param encoding_format [String] The format to return the embeddings in. Can be either float or base64.
61:     # @param user [String] A unique identifier representing your end-user
62:     # @return [Langchain::LLM::Response::OpenAIResponse] Response object
63:     def embed(
64:       text:,
65:       model: defaults[:embedding_model],
66:       encoding_format: nil,
67:       user: nil,
68:       dimensions: @defaults[:dimensions]
69:     )
70:       raise ArgumentError.new("text argument is required") if text.empty?
71:       raise ArgumentError.new("model argument is required") if model.empty?
72:       raise ArgumentError.new("encoding_format must be either float or base64") if encoding_format && %w[float base64].include?(encoding_format)
73: 
74:       parameters = {
75:         input: text,
76:         model: model
77:       }
78:       parameters[:encoding_format] = encoding_format if encoding_format
79:       parameters[:user] = user if user
80: 
81:       if dimensions
82:         parameters[:dimensions] = dimensions
83:       elsif EMBEDDING_SIZES.key?(model)
84:         parameters[:dimensions] = EMBEDDING_SIZES[model]
85:       end
86:

3: module Langchain::LLM
4:   #
5:   # Wrapper around Anthropic APIs.
6:   #
7:   # Gem requirements:
8:   #   gem "anthropic", "~> 1.10.0"
9:   #
10:   # Usage:
11:   #     llm = Langchain::LLM::Anthropic.new(api_key: ENV["ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"])
12:   #
13:   class Anthropic < Base
14:     DEFAULTS = {
15:       temperature: 0.0,
16:       completion_model: "claude-2.1",
17:       chat_model: "claude-sonnet-4-6",
18:       max_tokens: 256
19:     }.freeze
20: 
21:     # Initialize an Anthropic LLM instance
22:     #
23:     # @param api_key [String] The API key to use
24:     # @param llm_options [Hash] Options to pass to the Anthropic client
25:     # @param default_options [Hash] Default options to use on every call to LLM, e.g.: { temperature:, completion_model:, chat_model:, max_tokens:, thinking: }
26:     # @return [Langchain::LLM::Anthropic] Langchain::LLM::Anthropic instance
27:     def initialize(api_key:, llm_options: {}, default_options: {})
28:       depends_on "anthropic"
29: 
30:       @client = ::Anthropic::Client.new(api_key: api_key, **llm_options)
31:       @defaults = DEFAULTS.merge(default_options)
32:       chat_parameters.update(
33:         model: {default: @defaults[:chat_model]},
34:         temperature: {default: @defaults[:temperature]},
35:         max_tokens: {default: @defaults[:max_tokens]},
36:         metadata: {},
37:         system: {},
38:         thinking: {default: @defaults[:thinking]},
39:         request_options: {}
40:       )
41:       chat_parameters.ignore(:n, :user)
42:       chat_parameters.remap(stop: :stop_sequences)
43:     end
44: 
45:     # Generate a completion for a given prompt
46:     #
47:     # @param prompt [String] Prompt to generate a completion for
48:     # @param model [String] The model to use
49:     # @param max_tokens_to_sample [Integer] The maximum number of tokens to sample
50:     # @param stop_sequences [Array<String>] The stop sequences to use
51:     # @param temperature [Float] The temperature to use
52:     # @param top_p [Float] The top p value to use
53:     # @param top_k [Integer] The top k value to use
54:     # @param metadata [Hash] The metadata to use
55:     # @param stream [Boolean] Whether to stream the response
56:     # @return [Langchain::LLM::Response::AnthropicResponse] The completion
57:     def complete(
58:       prompt:,
59:       model: @defaults[:completion_model],
60:       max_tokens: @defaults[:max_tokens],
61:       stop_sequences: nil,
62:       temperature: @defaults[:temperature],
63:       top_p: nil,
64:       top_k: nil,
65:       metadata: nil,
66:       stream: nil
67:     )
68:       raise ArgumentError.new("model argument is required") if model.empty?
69:       raise ArgumentError.new("max_tokens argument is required") if max_tokens.nil?
70: 
71:       parameters = {
72:         model: model,
73:         prompt: prompt,
74:         max_tokens_to_sample: max_tokens,
75:         temperature: temperature
76:       }
77:       parameters[:stop_sequences] = stop_sequences if stop_sequences
78:       parameters[:top_p] = top_p if top_p
79:       parameters[:top_k] = top_k if top_k
80:       parameters[:metadata] = metadata if metadata
81:       parameters[:stream] = stream if stream
82: 
83:       response = with_api_error_handling do
84:         client.complete(parameters: parameters)
85:       end
86: 
87:       Langchain::LLM::Response::AnthropicResponse.new(response)
88:     end
89: 
90:     # Generate a chat completion for given messages
91:     #
92:     # @param [Hash] params unified chat parmeters from [Langchain::LLM::Parameters::Chat::SCHEMA]
93:     # @option params [Array<String>] :messages Input messages
94:     # @option params [String] :model The model that will complete your prompt
95:     # @option params [Integer] :max_tokens Maximum number of tokens to generate before stopping
96:     # @option params [Hash] :metadata Object describing metadata about the request
97:     # @option params [Array<String>] :stop_sequences Custom text sequences that will cause the model to stop generating
98:     # @option params [Boolean] :stream Whether to incrementally stream the response using server-sent events
99:     # @option params [String] :system System prompt
100:     # @option params [Float] :temperature Amount of randomness injected into the response
101:     # @option params [Array<String>] :tools Definitions of tools that the model may use
102:     # @option params [Hash] :thinking Enable extended thinking mode, e.g. { type: "enabled", budget_tokens: 4000 }
103:     # @option params [Integer] :top_k Only sample from the top K options for each subsequent token
104:     # @option params [Float] :top_p Use nucleus sampling.
105:     # @return [Langchain::LLM::Response::AnthropicResponse] The chat completion
106:     def chat(params = {}, &block)
107:       parameters = chat_parameters.to_params(params)
108: 
109:       raise ArgumentError.new("messages argument is required") if Array(parameters[:messages]).empty?
110:       raise ArgumentError.new("model argument is required") if parameters[:model].empty?
111:       raise ArgumentError.new("max_tokens argument is required") if parameters[:max_tokens].nil?
112: 
113:       if block
114:         @response_chunks = []
115:         parameters[:stream] = proc do |chunk|
116:           @response_chunks << chunk
117:           yield chunk
118:         end
119:       end
120: 
121:       response = client.messages.create(parameters)
122: 
123:       response = response_from_chunks if block
124:       reset_response_chunks
125: 
126:       Langchain::LLM::Response::AnthropicResponse.new(response)
127:     end
128: 
129:     def with_api_error_handling
130:       response = yield
131:       return if response.empty?
132: 
133:       raise Langchain::LLM::ApiError.new "Anthropic API error: #{response.dig("error", "message")}" if response&.dig("error")
134: 
135:       response
136:     end
137: 
138:     def response_from_chunks
139:       grouped_chunks = @response_chunks.group_by { |chunk| chunk["index"] }.except(nil)
140: 
141:       usage_chunk = @response_chunks.find { |chunk| chunk["type"] == "message_delta" }
142:       usage = usage_chunk&.dig("usage")&.transform_keys(&:to_sym)
143:       stop_reason = usage_chunk&.dig("delta", "stop_reason")
144: 
145:       content = grouped_chunks.map do |_index, chunks|
146:         text = chunks.map { |chunk| chunk.dig("delta", "text") }.join
147:         if !text.nil? && !text.empty?
148:           {type: "text", text: text}
149:         else
150:           tool_calls_from_choice_chunks(chunks)
151:         end
152:       end.flatten
153: 
154:       first_chunk = @response_chunks.first
155:       {
156:         id: first_chunk&.dig("id") || first_chunk&.dig("message", "id"),
157:         model: first_chunk&.dig("model") || first_chunk&.dig("message", "model"),
158:         content: content,
159:         usage: usage,
160:         role: "assistant",
161:         stop_reason: stop_reason
162:       }
163:     end
164: 
165:     def tool_calls_from_choice_chunks(chunks)
166:       return unless (first_block = chunks.find { |chunk| chunk.dig("content_block", "type") == "tool_use" })
167: 
168:       chunks.group_by { |chunk| chunk["index"] }.map do |index, chunks|
169:         input = chunks.select { |chunk| chunk.dig("delta", "partial_json") }
170:           .map! { |chunk| chunk.dig("delta", "partial_json") }.join
171:         {
172:           id: first_block.dig("content_block", "id"),
173:           type: "tool_use",
174:           name: first_block.dig("content_block", "name"),
175:           input: input.empty? ? nil : JSON.parse(input).transform_keys(&:to_sym)
176:         }
177:       end.compact
178:     end
179: 
180:     private
181: 
182:     def reset_response_chunks
183:       @response_chunks = []
184:     end
185:   end
186: end

69:     #
70:     # Generate the completion for a given prompt
71:     #
72:     # @param prompt [String] The prompt to complete
73:     # @param model [String] The model to use
74:     #   For a list of valid parameters and values, see:
75:     #   https://github.com/jmorganca/ollama/blob/main/docs/modelfile.md#valid-parameters-and-values
76:     # @option block [Proc] Receive the intermediate responses as a stream of +OllamaResponse+ objects.

84:     def complete(
85:       prompt:,
86:       model: defaults[:completion_model],
87:       images: nil,
88:       format: nil,
89:       system: nil,
90:       template: nil,
91:       context: nil,
92:       raw: nil,
93:       mirostat: nil,
94:       mirostat_eta: nil,
95:       mirostat_tau: nil,
96:       num_ctx: nil,
97:       num_gqa: nil,
98:       num_gpu: nil,
99:       num_thread: nil,
100:       repeat_last_n: nil,
101:       repeat_penalty: nil,
102:       temperature: defaults[:temperature],
103:       seed: nil,
104:       stop: nil,
105:       tfs_z: nil,
106:       num_predict: nil,
107:       top_k: nil,
108:       top_p: nil,
109:       stop_sequences: nil,
110:       &block
111:     )
112:       if stop_sequences
113:         stop = stop_sequences
114:       end
115: 
116:       parameters = {
117:         prompt: prompt,
118:         model: model,
119:         images: images,
120:         format: format,
121:         system: system,
122:         template: template,
123:         context: context,
124:         stream: block_given?, # rubocop:disable Performance/BlockGivenWithExplicitBlock
125:         raw: raw
126:       }.compact
127: 
128:       llm_parameters = {
129:         mirostat: mirostat,
130:         mirostat_eta: mirostat_eta,
131:         mirostat_tau: mirostat_tau,
132:         num_ctx: num_ctx,
133:         num_gqa: num_gqa,
134:         num_gpu: num_gpu,
135:         num_thread: num_thread,
136:         repeat_last_n: repeat_last_n,
137:         repeat_penalty: repeat_penalty,
138:         temperature: temperature,
139:         seed: seed,
140:         stop: stop,
141:         tfs_z: tfs_z,
142:         num_predict: num_predict,
143:         top_k: top_k,
144:         top_p: top_p
145:       }

147:       parameters[:options] = llm_parameters.compact
148:       responses_stream = []
149: 
150:       client.post("api/generate", parameters) do |req|
151:         req.options.on_data = json_responses_chunk_handler do |parsed_chunk|
152:           responses_stream << parsed_chunk
153:

161:     # Generate a chat completion
162:     #
163:     # @param messages [Array] The chat messages
164:     # @param model [String] The model to use
165:     # @param params [Hash] Unified chat parmeters from [Langchain::LLM::Parameters::Chat::SCHEMA]
166:     # @option params [Array<Hash>] :messages Array of messages
167:     # @option params [String] :model Model name
168:     # @option params [String] :format Format to return a response in. Currently the only accepted value is `json`
169:     # @option params [Float] :temperature The temperature to use
170:     # @option params [String] :template The prompt template to use (overrides what is defined in the `Modelfile`)
171:     # @option block [Proc] Receive the intermediate responses as a stream of +OllamaResponse+ objects.
172:     # @return [Langchain::LLM::Response::OllamaResponse] Response object
173:     #
174:     # Example:
175:     #
176:     #  final_resp = ollama.chat(messages:) { |resp| print resp.chat_completion }
177:     #  final_resp.total_tokens
178:     #
179:     # The message object has the following fields:
180:     #   role: the role of the message, either system, user or assistant
181:     #   content: the content of the message
182:     #   images (optional): a list of images to include in the message (for multimodal models such as llava)
183:     def chat(messages:, model: nil, **params, &block)
184:       parameters = chat_parameters.to_params(params.merge(messages:, model:, stream: block_given?)) # rubocop:disable Performance/BlockGivenWithExplicitBlock
185:       responses_stream = []
186: 
187:       client.post("api/chat", parameters) do |req|
188:         req.options.on_data = json_responses_chunk_handler do |parsed_chunk|
189:           responses_stream << parsed_chunk
190: 
191:           block&.call(Langchain::LLM::Response::OllamaResponse.new(parsed_chunk, model: parameters[:model]))
192:         end
193:       end
194: 
195:       generate_final_chat_completion_response(responses_stream, parameters[:model])
196:     end

198:     #
199:     # Generate an embedding for a given text
200:     #

202:     # @param model [String] The model to use
203:     # @param options [Hash] The options to use
204:     # @return [Langchain::LLM::Response::OllamaResponse] Response object
205:     #
206:     def embed(
207:       text:,
208:       model: defaults[:embedding_model],
209:       mirostat: nil,
210:       mirostat_eta: nil,
211:       mirostat_tau: nil,
212:       num_ctx: nil,
213:       num_gqa: nil,
214:       num_gpu: nil,
215:       num_thread: nil,
216:       repeat_last_n: nil,
217:       repeat_penalty: nil,
218:       temperature: defaults[:temperature],
219:       seed: nil,
220:       stop: nil,
221:       tfs_z: nil,
222:       num_predict: nil,
223:       top_k: nil,
224:       top_p: nil
225:     )
226:       parameters = {
227:         model: model,
228:         input: Array(text)
229:       }.compact
230: 
231:       llm_parameters = {
232:         mirostat: mirostat,
233:         mirostat_eta: mirostat_eta,
234:         mirostat_tau: mirostat_tau,
235:         num_ctx: num_ctx,
236:         num_gqa: num_gqa,
237:         num_gpu: num_gpu,
238:         num_thread: num_thread,
239:         repeat_last_n: repeat_last_n,
240:         repeat_penalty: repeat_penalty,
241:         temperature: temperature,
242:         seed: seed,
243:         stop: stop,
244:         tfs_z: tfs_z,
245:         num_predict: num_predict,
246:         top_k: top_k,
247:         top_p: top_p
248:       }
249: 
250:       parameters[:options] = llm_parameters.compact
251: 
252:       response = client.post("api/embed") do |req|
253:         req.body = parameters
254:       end
255: 
256:       Langchain::LLM::Response::OllamaResponse.new(response.body, model: parameters[:model])
257:     end

259:     # Generate a summary for a given text
260:     #
261:     # @param text [String] The text to generate a summary for
262:     # @return [String] The summary

265:         file_path: Langchain.root.join("langchain/llm/prompts/ollama/summarize_template.yaml")
266:       )
267:       prompt = prompt_template.format(text: text)
268: 
269:       complete(prompt: prompt)
270:     end
271: 
272:     private
273: 
274:     def client
275:       @client ||= Faraday.new(url: url, headers: auth_headers) do |conn|
276:         conn.request :json
277:         conn.response :json
278:         conn.response :raise_error
279:         conn.response :logger, Langchain.logger, {headers: true, bodies: true, errors: true}
280:       end
281:     end

283:     def auth_headers
284:       return unless @api_key
285: 
286:       {"Authorization" => "Bearer #{@api_key}"}
287:     end

289:     def json_responses_chunk_handler(&block)
290:       proc do |chunk, _size|
291:         chunk.split("\n").each do |chunk_line|
292:           parsed_chunk = JSON.parse(chunk_line)
293:           block.call(parsed_chunk)
294:         end
295:       end
296:     end

300:         "response" => responses_stream.map { |resp| resp["response"] }.join
301:       )
302:

309:       final_response["message"]["content"] = responses_stream.map { |resp| resp.dig("message", "content") }.join
310:

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Langchain::LLM
4:   # LLM interface for Aws Bedrock APIs: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/
5:   #
6:   # Gem requirements:
7:   #    gem 'aws-sdk-bedrockruntime', '~> 1.1'
8:   #
9:   # Usage:
10:   #    llm = Langchain::LLM::AwsBedrock.new(default_options: {})
11:   #
12:   class AwsBedrock < Base
13:     DEFAULTS = {
14:       chat_model: "anthropic.claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620-v1:0",
15:       completion_model: "anthropic.claude-v2:1",
16:       embedding_model: "amazon.titan-embed-text-v1",
17:       max_tokens_to_sample: 300,
18:       temperature: 1,
19:       top_k: 250,
20:       top_p: 0.999,
21:       stop_sequences: ["\n\nHuman:"],
22:       return_likelihoods: "NONE"
23:     }.freeze
24: 
25:     attr_reader :client, :defaults
26: 
27:     SUPPORTED_COMPLETION_PROVIDERS = %i[
28:       anthropic
29:       ai21
30:       cohere
31:       meta
32:     ].freeze
33: 
34:     SUPPORTED_CHAT_COMPLETION_PROVIDERS = %i[
35:       anthropic
36:       ai21
37:       mistral
38:     ].freeze
39: 
40:     SUPPORTED_EMBEDDING_PROVIDERS = %i[
41:       amazon
42:       cohere
43:     ].freeze
44: 
45:     def initialize(aws_client_options: {}, default_options: {})
46:       depends_on "aws-sdk-bedrockruntime", req: "aws-sdk-bedrockruntime"
47: 
48:       @client = ::Aws::BedrockRuntime::Client.new(**aws_client_options)
49:       @defaults = DEFAULTS.merge(default_options)
50: 
51:       chat_parameters.update(
52:         model: {default: @defaults[:chat_model]},
53:         temperature: {},
54:         max_tokens: {default: @defaults[:max_tokens_to_sample]},
55:         metadata: {},
56:         system: {}
57:       )
58:       chat_parameters.ignore(:n, :user)
59:       chat_parameters.remap(stop: :stop_sequences)
60:     end
61: 
62:     #
63:     # Generate an embedding for a given text
64:     #
65:     # @param text [String] The text to generate an embedding for
66:     # @param params extra parameters passed to Aws::BedrockRuntime::Client#invoke_model
67:     # @return [Langchain::LLM::AwsTitanResponse] Response object
68:     #
69:     def embed(text:, **params)
70:       raise "Completion provider #{embedding_provider} is not supported." unless SUPPORTED_EMBEDDING_PROVIDERS.include?(embedding_provider)
71: 
72:       parameters = compose_embedding_parameters params.merge(text:)
73: 
74:       response = client.invoke_model({
75:         model_id: @defaults[:embedding_model],
76:         body: parameters.to_json,
77:         content_type: "application/json",
78:         accept: "application/json"
79:       })
80: 
81:       parse_embedding_response response
82:     end
83: 
84:     #
85:     # Generate a completion for a given prompt
86:     #
87:     # @param prompt [String] The prompt to generate a completion for
88:     # @param params  extra parameters passed to Aws::BedrockRuntime::Client#invoke_model
89:     # @return [Langchain::LLM::Response::AnthropicResponse], [Langchain::LLM::Response::CohereResponse] or [Langchain::LLM::AI21Response] Response object
90:     #
91:     def complete(
92:       prompt:,
93:       model: @defaults[:completion_model],
94:       **params
95:     )
96:       raise "Completion provider #{model} is not supported." unless SUPPORTED_COMPLETION_PROVIDERS.include?(provider_name(model))
97: 
98:       parameters = compose_parameters(params, model)
99: 
100:       parameters[:prompt] = wrap_prompt prompt
101: 
102:       response = client.invoke_model({
103:         model_id: model,
104:         body: parameters.to_json,
105:         content_type: "application/json",
106:         accept: "application/json"
107:       })
108: 
109:       parse_response(response, model)
110:     end
111: 
112:     # Generate a chat completion for a given prompt
113:     # Currently only configured to work with the Anthropic provider and
114:     # the claude-3 model family
115:     #
116:     # @param [Hash] params unified chat parmeters from [Langchain::LLM::Parameters::Chat::SCHEMA]
117:     # @option params [Array<String>] :messages The messages to generate a completion for
118:     # @option params [String] :system The system prompt to provide instructions
119:     # @option params [String] :model The model to use for completion defaults to @defaults[:chat_model]
120:     # @option params [Integer] :max_tokens The maximum number of tokens to generate defaults to @defaults[:max_tokens_to_sample]
121:     # @option params [Array<String>] :stop The stop sequences to use for completion
122:     # @option params [Array<String>] :stop_sequences The stop sequences to use for completion
123:     # @option params [Float] :temperature The temperature to use for completion
124:     # @option params [Float] :top_p Use nucleus sampling.
125:     # @option params [Integer] :top_k Only sample from the top K options for each subsequent token
126:     # @yield [Hash] Provides chunks of the response as they are received
127:     # @return [Langchain::LLM::Response::AnthropicResponse] Response object
128:     def chat(params = {}, &block)
129:       parameters = chat_parameters.to_params(params)
130:       parameters = compose_parameters(parameters, parameters[:model])
131: 
132:       unless SUPPORTED_CHAT_COMPLETION_PROVIDERS.include?(provider_name(parameters[:model]))
133:         raise "Chat provider #{parameters[:model]} is not supported."
134:       end
135: 
136:       if block
137:         response_chunks = []
138: 
139:         client.invoke_model_with_response_stream(
140:           model_id: parameters[:model],
141:           body: parameters.except(:model).to_json,
142:           content_type: "application/json",
143:           accept: "application/json"
144:         ) do |stream|
145:           stream.on_event do |event|
146:             chunk = JSON.parse(event.bytes)
147:             response_chunks << chunk
148: 
149:             yield chunk
150:           end
151:         end
152: 
153:         response_from_chunks(response_chunks)
154:       else
155:         response = client.invoke_model({
156:           model_id: parameters[:model],
157:           body: parameters.except(:model).to_json,
158:           content_type: "application/json",
159:           accept: "application/json"
160:         })
161: 
162:         parse_response(response, parameters[:model])
163:       end
164:     end
165: 
166:     private
167: 
168:     def parse_model_id(model_id)
169:       model_id
170:         .gsub(/^(us|eu|apac|us-gov)\./, "") # Meta append "us." to their model ids, and AWS region prefixes are used by Bedrock cross-region model IDs.
171:         .split(".")
172:     end
173: 
174:     def provider_name(model_id)
175:       parse_model_id(model_id).first.to_sym
176:     end
177: 
178:     def model_name(model_id)
179:       parse_model_id(model_id).last
180:     end
181: 
182:     def completion_provider
183:       @defaults[:completion_model].split(".").first.to_sym
184:     end
185: 
186:     def embedding_provider
187:       @defaults[:embedding_model].split(".").first.to_sym
188:     end
189: 
190:     def wrap_prompt(prompt)
191:       if completion_provider == :anthropic
192:         "\n\nHuman: #{prompt}\n\nAssistant:"
193:       else
194:         prompt
195:       end
196:     end
197: 
198:     def max_tokens_key
199:       if completion_provider == :anthropic
200:         :max_tokens_to_sample
201:       elsif completion_provider == :cohere
202:         :max_tokens
203:       elsif completion_provider == :ai21
204:         :maxTokens
205:       end
206:     end
207: 
208:     def compose_parameters(params, model_id)
209:       if provider_name(model_id) == :anthropic
210:         compose_parameters_anthropic(params)
211:       elsif provider_name(model_id) == :cohere
212:         compose_parameters_cohere(params)
213:       elsif provider_name(model_id) == :ai21
214:         params
215:       elsif provider_name(model_id) == :meta
216:         params
217:       elsif provider_name(model_id) == :mistral
218:         params
219:       end
220:     end
221: 
222:     def compose_embedding_parameters(params)
223:       if embedding_provider == :amazon
224:         compose_embedding_parameters_amazon params
225:       elsif embedding_provider == :cohere
226:         compose_embedding_parameters_cohere params
227:       end
228:     end
229: 
230:     def parse_response(response, model_id)
231:       if provider_name(model_id) == :anthropic
232:         Langchain::LLM::Response::AwsBedrockAnthropicResponse.new(JSON.parse(response.body.string))
233:       elsif provider_name(model_id) == :cohere
234:         Langchain::LLM::Response::CohereResponse.new(JSON.parse(response.body.string))
235:       elsif provider_name(model_id) == :ai21
236:         Langchain::LLM::Response::AI21Response.new(JSON.parse(response.body.string, symbolize_names: true))
237:       elsif provider_name(model_id) == :meta
238:         Langchain::LLM::Response::AwsBedrockMetaResponse.new(JSON.parse(response.body.string))
239:       elsif provider_name(model_id) == :mistral
240:         Langchain::LLM::Response::MistralAIResponse.new(JSON.parse(response.body.string))
241:       end
242:     end
243: 
244:     def parse_embedding_response(response)
245:       json_response = JSON.parse(response.body.string)
246: 
247:       if embedding_provider == :amazon
248:         Langchain::LLM::Response::AwsTitanResponse.new(json_response)
249:       elsif embedding_provider == :cohere
250:         Langchain::LLM::Response::CohereResponse.new(json_response)
251:       end
252:     end
253: 
254:     def compose_embedding_parameters_amazon(params)
255:       default_params = @defaults.merge(params)
256: 
257:       {
258:         inputText: default_params[:text],
259:         dimensions: default_params[:dimensions],
260:         normalize: default_params[:normalize]
261:       }.compact
262:     end
263: 
264:     def compose_embedding_parameters_cohere(params)
265:       default_params = @defaults.merge(params)
266: 
267:       {
268:         texts: [default_params[:text]],
269:         truncate: default_params[:truncate],
270:         input_type: default_params[:input_type],
271:         embedding_types: default_params[:embedding_types]
272:       }.compact
273:     end
274: 
275:     def compose_parameters_cohere(params)
276:       default_params = @defaults.merge(params)
277: 
278:       {
279:         max_tokens: default_params[:max_tokens_to_sample],
280:         temperature: default_params[:temperature],
281:         p: default_params[:top_p],
282:         k: default_params[:top_k],
283:         stop_sequences: default_params[:stop_sequences]
284:       }
285:     end
286: 
287:     def compose_parameters_anthropic(params)
288:       params.merge(anthropic_version: "bedrock-2023-05-31")
289:     end
290: 
291:     def response_from_chunks(chunks)
292:       raw_response = {}
293: 
294:       chunks.group_by { |chunk| chunk["type"] }.each do |type, chunks|
295:         case type
296:         when "message_start"
297:           raw_response = chunks.first["message"]
298:         when "content_block_start"
299:           raw_response["content"] = chunks.map { |chunk| chunk["content_block"] }
300:         when "content_block_delta"
301:           chunks.group_by { |chunk| chunk["index"] }.each do |index, deltas|
302:             deltas.group_by { |delta| delta.dig("delta", "type") }.each do |type, deltas|
303:               case type
304:               when "text_delta"
305:                 raw_response["content"][index]["text"] = deltas.map { |delta| delta.dig("delta", "text") }.join
306:               when "input_json_delta"
307:                 json_string = deltas.map { |delta| delta.dig("delta", "partial_json") }.join
308:                 raw_response["content"][index]["input"] = json_string.empty? ? {} : JSON.parse(json_string)
309:               end
310:             end
311:           end
312:         when "message_delta"
313:           chunks.each do |chunk|
314:             raw_response = raw_response.merge(chunk["delta"])
315:             raw_response["usage"] = raw_response["usage"].merge(chunk["usage"]) if chunk["usage"]
316:           end
317:         end
318:       end
319: 
320:       Langchain::LLM::Response::AwsBedrockAnthropicResponse.new(raw_response)
321:     end
322:   end
323: end

3: #
4: # Extends a class to be used as a tool in the assistant.
5: # A tool is a collection of functions (methods) used to perform specific tasks.
6: #
7: # == Usage
8: #
9: # 1. Extend your class with {Langchain::ToolDefinition}
10: # 2. Use {#define_function} to define each function of the tool
11: #
12: # == Key Concepts
13: #
14: # - {#define_function}: Defines a new function (method) for the tool
15: # - {ParameterBuilder#property}: Defines properties for the function parameters
16: # - {ParameterBuilder#item}: Alias for {ParameterBuilder#property}, used for array items
17: #
18: # These methods support various data types and nested structures, allowing for flexible and expressive tool definitions.
19: #
20: # @example Defining a tool with various property types and configurations
21: #   define_function :sample_function, description: "Demonstrates various property types and configurations" do
22: #     property :string_prop, type: "string", description: "A simple string property"
23: #     property :number_prop, type: "number", description: "A number property"
24: #     property :integer_prop, type: "integer", description: "An integer property"
25: #     property :boolean_prop, type: "boolean", description: "A boolean property"
26: #     property :enum_prop, type: "string", description: "An enum property", enum: ["option1", "option2", "option3"]
27: #     property :required_prop, type: "string", description: "A required property", required: true
28: #     property :array_prop, type: "array", description: "An array property" do
29: #       item type: "string", description: "Array item"
30: #     end
31: #     property :object_prop, type: "object", description: "An object property" do
32: #       property :nested_string, type: "string", description: "Nested string property"
33: #       property :nested_number, type: "number", description: "Nested number property"
34: #     end
35: #   end
36: #
37: module Langchain::ToolDefinition
38:   # Defines a function for the tool
39:   #
40:   # @param method_name [Symbol] Name of the method to define
41:   # @param description [String] Description of the function
42:   # @yield Block that defines the parameters for the function
43:   def define_function(method_name, description:, &block)
44:     function_schemas.add_function(method_name:, description:, &block)
45:   end
46: 
47:   # Returns the FunctionSchemas instance for this tool
48:   #
49:   # @return [FunctionSchemas] The FunctionSchemas instance
50:   def function_schemas
51:     @function_schemas ||= FunctionSchemas.new(tool_name)
52:   end
53: 
54:   # Returns the snake_case version of the class name as the tool's name
55:   #
56:   # @return [String] The snake_case version of the class name
57:   def tool_name
58:     @tool_name ||= name
59:       .gsub("::", "_")
60:       .gsub(/(?<=[A-Z])(?=[A-Z][a-z])|(?<=[a-z\d])(?=[A-Z])/, "_")
61:       .downcase
62:   end
63: 
64:   def self.extended(base)
65:     base.include(InstanceMethods)
66:   end
67: 
68:   module InstanceMethods
69:     # Create a tool response
70:     # @param content [String, nil] The content of the tool response
71:     # @param image_url [String, nil] The URL of an image
72:     # @return [Langchain::ToolResponse] The tool response
73:     def tool_response(content: nil, image_url: nil)
74:       Langchain::ToolResponse.new(content: content, image_url: image_url)
75:     end
76:   end
77: 
78:   # Manages schemas for functions
79:   class FunctionSchemas
80:     def initialize(tool_name)
81:       @schemas = {}
82:       @tool_name = tool_name
83:     end
84: 
85:     # Adds a function to the schemas
86:     #
87:     # @param method_name [Symbol] Name of the method to add
88:     # @param description [String] Description of the function
89:     # @yield Block that defines the parameters for the function
90:     # @raise [ArgumentError] If a block is defined and no parameters are specified for the function
91:     def add_function(method_name:, description:, &block)
92:       name = "#{@tool_name}__#{method_name}"
93: 
94:       if block_given? # rubocop:disable Performance/BlockGivenWithExplicitBlock
95:         parameters = ParameterBuilder.new(parent_type: "object").build(&block)
96: 
97:         if parameters[:properties].empty?
98:           raise ArgumentError, "Function parameters must have at least one property defined within it, if a block is provided"
99:         end
100:       end
101: 
102:       @schemas[method_name] = {
103:         type: "function",
104:         function: {name:, description:, parameters:}.compact
105:       }
106:     end
107: 
108:     # Converts schemas to OpenAI-compatible format
109:     #
110:     # @return [String] JSON string of schemas in OpenAI format
111:     def to_openai_format
112:       @schemas.values
113:     end
114: 
115:     # Converts schemas to Anthropic-compatible format
116:     #
117:     # @return [String] JSON string of schemas in Anthropic format
118:     def to_anthropic_format
119:       @schemas.values.map do |schema|
120:         # Adds a default input_schema if no parameters are present
121:         schema[:function][:parameters] ||= {
122:           type: "object",
123:           properties: {},
124:           required: []
125:         }
126: 
127:         schema[:function].transform_keys(parameters: :input_schema)
128:       end
129:     end
130: 
131:     # Converts schemas to Google Gemini-compatible format
132:     #
133:     # @return [String] JSON string of schemas in Google Gemini format
134:     def to_google_gemini_format
135:       @schemas.values.map { |schema| schema[:function] }
136:     end
137:   end
138: 
139:   # Builds parameter schemas for functions
140:   class ParameterBuilder
141:     VALID_TYPES = %w[object array string number integer boolean].freeze
142: 
143:     def initialize(parent_type:)
144:       @schema = (parent_type == "object") ? {type: "object", properties: {}, required: []} : {}
145:       @parent_type = parent_type
146:     end
147: 
148:     # Builds the parameter schema
149:     #
150:     # @yield Block that defines the properties of the schema
151:     # @return [Hash] The built schema
152:     def build(&block)
153:       instance_eval(&block)
154:       @schema
155:     end
156: 
157:     # Defines a property in the schema
158:     #
159:     # @param name [Symbol] Name of the property (required only for a parent of type object)
160:     # @param type [String] Type of the property
161:     # @param description [String] Description of the property
162:     # @param enum [Array] Array of allowed values
163:     # @param required [Boolean] Whether the property is required
164:     # @yield [Block] Block for nested properties (only for object and array types)
165:     # @raise [ArgumentError] If any parameter is invalid
166:     def property(name = nil, type:, description: nil, enum: nil, required: false, &block)
167:       validate_parameters(name:, type:, enum:, required:)
168: 
169:       prop = {type:, description:, enum:}.compact
170: 
171:       if block_given? # rubocop:disable Performance/BlockGivenWithExplicitBlock
172:         nested_schema = ParameterBuilder.new(parent_type: type).build(&block)
173: 
174:         case type
175:         when "object"
176:           if nested_schema[:properties].empty?
177:             raise ArgumentError, "Object properties must have at least one property defined within it"
178:           end
179:           prop = nested_schema
180:         when "array"
181:           if nested_schema.empty?
182:             raise ArgumentError, "Array properties must have at least one item defined within it"
183:           end
184:           prop[:items] = nested_schema
185:         end
186:       end
187: 
188:       if @parent_type == "object"
189:         @schema[:properties][name] = prop
190:         @schema[:required] << name.to_s if required
191:       else
192:         @schema = prop
193:       end
194:     end
195: 
196:     # Alias for property method, used for defining array items
197:     alias_method :item, :property
198: 
199:     private
200: 
201:     # Validates the parameters for a property
202:     #
203:     # @param name [Symbol] Name of the property
204:     # @param type [String] Type of the property
205:     # @param enum [Array] Array of allowed values
206:     # @param required [Boolean] Whether the property is required
207:     # @raise [ArgumentError] If any parameter is invalid
208:     def validate_parameters(name:, type:, enum:, required:)
209:       if @parent_type == "object"
210:         if name.nil?
211:           raise ArgumentError, "Name must be provided for properties of an object"
212:         end
213:         unless name.is_a?(Symbol)
214:           raise ArgumentError, "Invalid name '#{name}'. Name must be a symbol"
215:         end
216:       end
217: 
218:       unless VALID_TYPES.include?(type)
219:         raise ArgumentError, "Invalid type '#{type}'. Valid types are: #{VALID_TYPES.join(", ")}"
220:       end
221: 
222:       unless enum.nil? || enum.is_a?(Array)
223:         raise ArgumentError, "Invalid enum '#{enum}'. Enum must be nil or an array"
224:       end
225: 
226:       unless [true, false].include?(required)
227:         raise ArgumentError, "Invalid required '#{required}'. Required must be a boolean"
228:       end
229:     end
230:   end
231: end

## Step 1: Orient in the codebase

This is **Langchain.rb**, a Ruby framework for LLM-powered apps (RAG, assistants). It lives in `/Users/luc/Developer/luuuc/oss/sense-benchmark/baseline/langchainrb`.

Code organization:

- **Assistant / tool-calling orchestration**: `lib/langchain/assistant.rb`
- **Provider-selection layer for the assistant**: `lib/langchain/assistant/llm/adapter.rb`
- **Per-provider assistant adapters**: `lib/langchain/assistant/llm/adapters/*.rb`
- **Shared adapter base**: `lib/langchain/assistant/llm/adapters/base.rb`
- **Per-provider message objects**: `lib/langchain/assistant/messages/*.rb`
- **Shared message base**: `lib/langchain/assistant/messages/base.rb`
- **Raw LLM clients**: `lib/langchain/llm/*.rb`
- **Shared LLM client base**: `lib/langchain/llm/base.rb`
- **Per-provider response wrappers**: `lib/langchain/llm/response/*.rb`
- **Shared response base**: `lib/langchain/llm/response/base_response.rb`
- **Unified chat parameter schema**: `lib/langchain/llm/parameters/chat.rb`
- **Tool schema formatting**: `lib/langchain/tool_definition.rb`
- **Tests mirror `lib/` under `spec/`**
- **Loading**: Rails Engine autoloading via `lib/langchain/engine.rb:5` (plus `zeitwerk` dependency in gemspec).

---

## Step 2: Trace how the assistant talks to a provider for a tool-calling turn

Execution order, from assistant down to provider-specific code:

1. `lib/langchain/assistant.rb:135` — `Assistant#run` starts the state machine.
2. `lib/langchain/assistant.rb:302` — `handle_user_or_tool_message` calls `chat_with_llm`.
3. `lib/langchain/assistant.rb:338` — `chat_with_llm` calls `@llm_adapter.build_chat_params(...)` at `:341` and then `@llm.chat(**params, &@block)` at `:348`.
4. `lib/langchain/assistant.rb:57` — `@llm_adapter` was set by `LLM::Adapter.build(llm)`.
5. `lib/langchain/assistant/llm/adapter.rb:8` — `Adapter.build` selects the provider-specific adapter class by `llm.class`.
6. Provider adapter’s `build_chat_params` (e.g. `lib/langchain/assistant/llm/adapters/openai.rb:16`) formats tools/messages/tool_choice for that provider.
7. The raw LLM client’s `chat` method (e.g. `lib/langchain/llm/openai.rb:122`) performs the HTTP/API call and wraps the raw result in a provider-specific response object (e.g. `lib/langchain/llm/openai.rb:148`).
8. Back in `lib/langchain/assistant.rb:305` — `add_message(role: response.role, content: response.chat_completion, tool_calls: response.tool_calls)` stores the result.
9. `lib/langchain/assistant.rb:311` — `set_state_for` inspects `response.tool_calls` to decide whether to execute tools.
10. `lib/langchain/assistant.rb:365` — `run_tool` calls `@llm_adapter.extract_tool_call_args(tool_call:)` to parse the provider’s tool-call shape.
11. `lib/langchain/assistant.rb:375` — the tool method is invoked; output is added back via `@llm_adapter.tool_role` at `:379`.

## Step 6: Find every site that must change to add a new tool-calling provider

To add a new provider (call it `NewProvider`) the following sites must change:

### 1. Raw LLM client

Create `lib/langchain/llm/new_provider.rb`:
- Class `Langchain::LLM::NewProvider < Base`
- `DEFAULTS` hash
- `initialize`
- `chat(params = {}, &block)` returning a `Langchain::LLM::Response::NewProviderResponse`
- Optional `embed`, `complete`, `summarize`

### 2. Response wrapper

Create `lib/langchain/llm/response/new_provider_response.rb`:
- Class `Langchain::LLM::Response::NewProviderResponse < BaseResponse`
- Implement `role`, `chat_completion`, `tool_calls`, `prompt_tokens`, `completion_tokens`, `total_tokens`, `completion`

### 3. Message class

Create `lib/langchain/assistant/messages/new_provider_message.rb`:
- Class `Langchain::Assistant::Messages::NewProviderMessage < Base`
- `ROLES` constant, `TOOL_ROLE` constant
- `initialize`, `to_hash`, `llm?`, `tool?`, `system?`, `user?`

### 4. Assistant adapter

Create `lib/langchain/assistant/llm/adapters/new_provider.rb`:
- Class `Langchain::Assistant::LLM::Adapters::NewProvider < Base`
- `build_chat_params`, `build_message`, `extract_tool_call_args`, `tool_role`, `support_system_message?`, `allowed_tool_choices`, `available_tool_names`, `build_tools`

### 5. Adapter selection

Modify `lib/langchain/assistant/llm/adapter.rb:8`:
- Add an `elsif llm.is_a?(Langchain::LLM::NewProvider)` branch returning `LLM::Adapters::NewProvider.new`.

### 6. Tool schema formatter (if provider needs a custom tool shape)

Modify `lib/langchain/tool_definition.rb`:
- Add a `to_new_provider_format` method in `FunctionSchemas` (`tool_definition.rb:79`) if the provider’s tool schema differs from OpenAI/Anthropic/Gemini.

### 7. Tests

Add/modify:

- `spec/lib/langchain/llm/new_provider_spec.rb`
- `spec/lib/langchain/llm/response/new_provider_response_spec.rb`
- `spec/lib/langchain/assistant/messages/new_provider_message_spec.rb`
- `spec/lib/langchain/assistant/llm/adapters/new_provider_spec.rb`
- Update `spec/lib/langchain/assistant/llm/adapter_spec.rb` to test selection of the new adapter.
- Add fixtures under `spec/fixtures/llm/new_provider/` (chat.json, chat_with_tool_calls.json, embed.json, etc.).

### 8. README / docs (optional but expected)

Update `README.md` supported providers list (~line 58).
